title: paeonia peregrina var. microcarpa
intro: Paeonia peregrina var. microcarpa, also known as the Himalayan peony, is a plant species within the genus Paeonia, characterized by its small size and unique characteristics. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Paeonia peregrina var. microcarpa include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of dried roots or as an extract, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
